text: This is a fine wedding. And to think, it's for us.
This ceremony is so different from an Orc marriage. Less blood.
I never would have dreamed I would be getting married in the Temple of Mara.
Weddings always make me cry. What am I supposed to do now that I'm the one getting married?
It's a simple ceremony, but it'll do. You're here. That's enough.
Who would have thought I would be getting married?
I don't want to see you ever again.
Get away from me. Liar!
You should leave. Now.
Get out. Leave me alone!
You're abandoning me? Me? I never want to see you ever again.
Get out of my sight.
I'll meet you there.
I'll see you there, then.
Very well. I'll meet you there.
All right. I'll see you there.
If that's what you want. I'll see you there, soon.
I'll see you there.
Then I'll see you at home!
Wise decision. The vaults in the Hall of the Vigilant will guard this against future temptation.
Bye, love.
Conquer your enemies, my love.
I'll be here if you need me, love.
You're going? I'll miss you, dear.
Leaving? Take the world by storm, dearest.
I'll be here, my dear.
You have quite a house. I'm thinking of setting up a small store here. Sell odds and ends.
Strange house, but strong. Solid. I think I'll open a store. A task to tend to whenever you're away.
This house of yours is wondrous, at least to these eyes. I've thought about starting a store. Something to do here when you're out.
This house is huge. I'll be tripping my feet all over this place. Hey, I think I'll start a little business here. Something to do, you know?
I've seen bigger houses, but it's yours, so I like it. Now, I think I need something to do if you're ever away, so I'll be opening a store.
A good house. You chose well. I don't like sitting still, so I've decided to open a store. Buy and sell whatever is needed.
It's good to see you again, dear.
It's pleasing to see you again.
Ah, it's my love, come to visit.
Hello, dear. I haven't been drinking too much, if that's what you're thinking.
You and I, we're the only people in Skyrim worth loving. That's what I think.
Good to see you, dear.
Do you need anything, my love?
Speak. You can ask anything of me, love.
Is there anything you need, dearest?
I love you. You know that, right?
What do you need, love? Ask anything.
Yes, love?
Ain't sure if they're takin' on new members, but you should talk to the Companions. They're over in their hall, Jorrvaskr.
If by "work" you mean "testing your steel in bloody battle."
Aye, that I do. Have all my life. We Battle-Borns have been here since the beginning. Along with the Gray-Manes.
Our families been close as kin for generations.
But Ulfric Stormcloak's uprising has divided us. Now seems we can scarce look at each other without comin' to blows. Petty and foolish, that.
They're sayin' dragons have returned to Skyrim. Now that there, that's a real problem.
Truly?
Ha! By Shor, this city could use more like you.
Best head up to Dragonsreach, then, and see the jarl. He's lord of Whiterun, and can set you on the path.
Ha! The most important question of all, that one.
You'll want the Bannered Mare. See old Hulda, she'll keep you swimmin' in mead.
But mind the bard, Mikael. Poncy little milk drinker, that one.
A pity. And words I hear too often.
But there's no shame in it. One of them dragons comes callin' in Whiterun, the place'll go up like tinder.
Ah, so it's gold you're after, then?
You need only ask about it. Plenty o' folk in Whiterun with too much fat and gold to be bothered doing anything for themselves.
Well met, kinsman. What brings you to Whiterun?
Well met, traveler. What brings you to Whiterun?
Ah, hello my Argonian friend. In the market for some hunting supplies?
Ah, hello my Khajiit friend. In the market for some hunting supplies?
Ah, kinsman. How my heart soars to look upon a brother Bosmer. Please, how can I serve your hunting needs?
Ah... How my heart soars to look upon a sister Bosmer. Please, how can I serve your hunting needs?
Hello, friend. In the market for some hunting supplies?
Well met, traveler.
...You! Why?
The citizens of Whiterun are forever in your debt!
In defeating these Stormcloak traitors, you have proven the hollowness of their cause and the fullness of your hearts.
They already sing of your valor and skill! The halls of Sovngarde are no doubt ringing with your praises!
Revel in your victory here today, even as the gods revel in your honor!
Thank you for your role in all of this.
It'd be my honor should you decide to make Whiterun your home. Speak with Proventus. He'll make the necessary arrangements.
Everyone, on me! The jarl wishes to speak!
Good.
You won't. The jarl has ordered a life sentence for you. Get comfortable in the dark, 'cause you'll never see the sun again.
We're not. The Silver-Bloods pay our wage. Every man here is a trained mercenary.
For Whiterun! For the Empire!
Now, you. Get down there.
All right, prisoner. Eyes front. You're in Cidhna Mine, now. And we expect you to earn your keep.
There's no resting your hide in a cell in this prison. Here, you work. You'll mine ore until you start throwing up silver bars.
You got it?
I said get down there. Now.
This is a jail, owned by the Silver-Blood family. We use the prisoners to mine for ore.
It's the most secure prison in Skyrim. Throw scum in, close the gates. No one gets out.
So unless you're here to dig, you don't belong here.
It's off to Cidhna Mine for you.
All right. Open her up.
Carry on men, my gratitude and blessings go with you!
Anything for my favorite lass.
Lest our honor be lessened should we allow these bloodthirsty beasts to prowl our lands!
Oh? Were you after her yourself? I won't stand in your way, my friend. You have my word.
Carlotta put you up to this, didn't she? I'm sorry, but that fiery widow is mine. She just doesn't know it yet.
Maybe you're right. I guess I just didn't want anyone to think I couldn't handle one lass.
On my honor, I won't bother Carlotta ever again.
But Ulfric will not stop here. No, he will continue to strike out against any true Nord who remains faithful to the Empire.
He will continue to sow discord and chaos wherever he can.
What did you just say? All I heard was the sound of jealousy.
Cidhna Mine's for prisoner scum only. Keep out.
Don't get smart with me. I'm in charge. You keep it up, I'll have your toes cut off.
We sure showed them!
They'll think twice before coming back here, that's for sure!
We did it!
I feel so alive!
We destroyed those Stormcloak traitors!
We showed those Imperials what real Nords are made of!
We've driven the Imperials out of Whiterun. This is good. Very good. We now control the center. It's a powerful position. One I aim to keep.
Here. Wouldn't want you going into a fight on an empty stomach. I'll have another meal ready tomorrow.
A warrior marches on the gut. Here. This should give you strength. I'll cook another tomorrow.
Here. This is from a recipe my people have passed on to me. I can cook another tomorrow.
Here you are. I added a little mead into the recipe. I think it tastes better. Let me know what you think tomorrow, and I'll make another one.
I should be asking you the same question, but all right. I'll cook. Here, try this, and don't you dare ask for another one before tomorrow.
I can. Here. This is fresh. I'll have another one made tomorrow.
A home for the two of us. I'll meet you there.
It will be our little nest. I'll be waiting for you there.
The two of us, living together. I'll see you at home!
I'll see you at home, then. Don't keep me waiting!
I'll be waiting for you at home, then.
I guess we have our entire lives together, now. Where should we live? You could stay with me...
We face the world together now. Where should we live? My home is yours.
We're united, but where should we live? My home is yours, of course.
I'm a married man! Hey, where should we live? I guess you could always stay with me.
This is the happiest day of my life. Where should we live? My home is your home now, of course.
Married. It doesn't feel like I thought it would, but I'm happy. Where should we live? You could stay with me...
We're quartered in the Hall of the Vigilant. Keeper Carcette heads Skyrim's branch of our order there, providing healing and justice as needed.
To the block, prisoner. Nice and easy.
Next, the Nord in the rags!
Forget the list. He goes to the block.
Follow the captain, prisoner.
As we commend your souls to Aetherius, blessings of the Eight Divines upon you, for you are the salt and earth of Nirn, our beloved...
It's nothing. Carry on.
Captain. What should we do? He's not on the list.
Who are you?
Ulfric Stormcloak. Some here in Helgen call you a hero. But a hero doesn't use a power like the Voice to murder his king and usurp his throne.
Wait a moment... do I detect a hint of genuine avarice from this noble thief?
Perhaps Karliah and Brynjolf misjudged you and your true nature is no different than my own.
He's no longer abusing the power of the Eye. I would've expected all this to stop.
And yet...
You've done it! You've beaten him!
Caves sounded more exciting in the bard's songs.
It's kind of dark in here.
How deep is this cave?
It feels like a whole mountain of rock is above  us.
Hard to believe people actually have to work in places like this.
Wonder what they mine from these walls.
I'll take a sword or mace over a pick axe any day.
Do you think we'll find gold nuggets in here?
Never knew a place could be so ancient. I bet it's over a thousand years old.
Wonder if any of my ancestors ever walked these halls?
Everything in here seems to be about death. Burial urns, crypts, funeral shrouds. It's a bit depressing.
I wonder what the ancient nords used this place for?
This place is amazing! It's hard to believe that mortal hands built them.
What do you think these devices were for?
The stories don't say why the dwarves disappeared. I wonder if their machines turned on them.
It's amazing this place is so well preserved. There isn't even a hint of rust.
The Jarls never want to pay to keep the forts. They just keep slipping further into ruin.
I wonder what great battles were fought here?
Do you think Tiber Septim ever fought here?
I can see why they built the fort here. A small force could control most of the nearby land.
Wizards. Why is it always wizards.
Most bandits are cowards. These seem to be braver than most.
I hate bandits and thieves. Men like these raid our town every year.
I hope that when I die, I don't end up as a shambling corpse.
The Forsworn are strange men. They fight to reclaim Markarth, but they live like savages.
I don't like wizards and their spells. Give me an axe any day.
These walking devices are strange yet amazing. What magic powers them?
Taken alone, the draugr aren't so fearsome. They're far more deadly in greater numbers.
These Forsworn are just savages. They live in caves and ruins when civilized folk build towns and cities.
I thought about becoming a bandit. I decided I preferred clean clothes and fresh mead.
These Forsworn don't even have the decency to dress right.
Draugr are slow and clumsy. Hardly a challenge for a man like me.
Don't get too far ahead.
Ah yes, the extract, good. Poisonous, of course, but it has its uses.
I suppose you want something for your trouble? Here.
Very well.
We have nothing to talk about. I can't believe what you did to me.
Your words are cheap. Why should I speak to you after what you've done?
And why should I speak with you? You broke my heart.
You. You should just go. I don't have enough ale to stomach talking to you.
Look at you. Come crawling back, after everything you've done to me?
What do you want? There's no words left to say to each other.
Thanks.
Get out of here. Get out of here and never come back!
What did you say? Get out of my sight!
You're a monster. Leave and never come back!
What'd you say to me? Get out! I never want to see you again.
You come all this way just to hurt me a second time? Get out of my sight!
Leave. Now!
Everything you see is grown at my family's own Hollyfrost Farm. Fresh and filling.
I also do a little herb growing. The court wizard, Wuunferth, pays us to plant a little Nightshade. Who knows what he uses it for?
Hey, could you deliver this bottle of Nightshade Extract to Wuunferth?
I am. I'd be glad to stand by your side until the Divines take us, if you'll have me.
I'll give you another chance, but watch yourself.
You want to try again? Fine, but I'll be watching you carefully.
Try again? You and me? Fine, but I got my eyes on you.
You're testing my patience, but very well. I'll give you another chance.
All right, we'll start again, but watch yourself.
An Amulet of Mara? You're not married? Surprising.
That amulet. I've seen the Nords wear these when they're looking to marry. With your strength, you should have no trouble.
Is that Lady Mara's amulet? I'm shocked a fine person like you isn't married already.
You're wearing Mara's amulet? You? I'd marry you. I'd marry you twice.
Look at you. Wearing an Amulet of Mara. Are you saying you're available?
An Amulet of Mara, you're looking for marriage, then?
You go ahead and arrange the wedding ceremony when you're ready.
You should speak to the priest in the Temple of Mara to arrange the ceremony. No sense waiting.
Have you spoken to the Temple of Mara, yet? I'm so excited.
Hey, shouldn't you tell the Temple of Mara that we're getting married? Be hard to have a wedding without a priest.
Now shouldn't you be off arranging our wedding day? Don't keep me waiting.
You should tell the priest we're getting married. They'll take things from there.
I'll see you at our wedding!
Our wedding is soon. I'll see you then!
Never thought I'd get married under a Nord god. But with you, it feels right.
Our wedding is soon, under Mara's loving eyes. It'll be perfect.
We have our wedding soon, don't we? That's right, we do!
I'm looking forward to our wedding. You and me...
The wedding is soon. It'll be a good day.
But we haven't taken a side because it's not our fight.
Maybe we'll pay you a visit tonight, little spy. We got ways of finding out what you really are.
Imperial spies? You can't be serious!
Hey, maybe the reason these gray-skins don't help in the war is because they're Imperial spies!
You come here where you're not wanted, you eat our food, you pollute our city with your stink, and you refuse to help the Stormcloaks.
Ever since that Imperial craven ran me through from behind, I've had trouble breathing. Don't have any trouble drinking, though.
Do come again!
Very well.
May you lose yourself in the pages of a good book!
As you travel Skyrim, make sure you stop and take in the view from time to time!
If you need another drink, I'll be right here.
Have a seat, there should be plenty of space.
Don't take anything for granted in life. Nothing lasts forever.
Be sure to stop by Sadri's Used Wares in the Gray Quarter.
Take care, friend.
Sometimes the difference between life an' death is a swift steed.
Sorry about the smell. It's a stable, after all.
Whether it's armor you need or a new blade, come see me.
If you meet anyone in your travels who needs good steel, send them my way.
Strength and steel are well and good, but magic is the true power in this world.
A strong sword arm wins battles, but a keen mind wins wars.
Safe journeys, sword-brother.
Farewell, then.
A most pleasant chat. Until the next!
I bid you safe travels.
Be seeing you.
Alright, then.
Good day to you.
If you're not doing something you enjoy, then you're wasting your life.
I hope to see you again soon.
If you have need of tonics or potions, come see me at my shop.
Watch yourself out there.
Battle reveals who a man really is. Remember that.
Keep on your guard. This city can be a little rough for those such as us.
It was a pleasure.
I should have been an adventurer like you, instead of a farmer.
Never trade a sword for a hoe, my friend.
Right. See you around.
Aye, till next time.
Spare a coin for a poor old woman?
A few septims for my supper is all I ask.
I ask only for a little charity.
Will you spare a few coins to help someone who's got nothing?
I ain't askin' for much, just a few septims.
The Divines smile on those who show mercy an' charity.
A few septims ain't nothing. You can spare that, can't you?
The Augur was a brilliant mage. Truly inspired. Mastered spells others could barely comprehend.
He was especially gifted when it came to Restoration. He's... Well, he's very particular about who shares the knowledge.
So you'll need his approval first. Go on, go talk to him. I'm sure he's been paying attention, and will be expecting you.
Oh, goodness no. There are those far more skilled than I.
Oh, didn't you know?
It looks like you're ready to speak with the Augur.
Look at you. Such a devoted student of Restoration.
It's comforting to see that not everyone has dismissed it as entirely as most members of the College. Truly comforting.
You are worthy. The knowledge is yours.
Faal Kel...?! Nikriinne...
Meyye! Tahrodiis aanne! Him hinde pah liiv! Zu'u hin daan!
You are banished!
No, damn you!
Which is why I brought the Elder Scroll.
You feel fear for the first time, worm. I see it in your eyes.
You are banished! Alduin, we shout you out from all our endings unto the last!
Begone, World-Eater! By words with older bones than your own we break your perch on this age and send you out!
If I die today, it will not be in terror.
Gormlaith! We're running out of time! The battle...
Together.
And mine is yours.
As short as life can be in this land, we will have each other.
Well, look at us, getting married. Life in Skyrim ain't all bad, huh?
Good. The two of us then. All of Skyrim should get ready to stand aside.
Then it's settled. You and me.
I see.
If that is your wish.
Very well. I am poorer for it.
What are you, some kind of tease? Stendarr's Mercy...
Waste of time, then.
Your choice.
You're strong. Clever. I'd be proud to face the challenges of life with you, if you feel the same way.
You have a kind heart. I'd be honored to walk by your side until the trees themselves fade away, if you would have me.
I'd be a fool to turn down a chance of marrying you. I'm game if you're game!
You are pleasant company, more than pleasant. And what about you? Would you... be interested in a life together?
Won't lie, I am. And you?
Grrah!
I doubt it can be repaired. You'll have to buy a new one.
My tunic is fading. Too much sun and salt spray.
If you give me a list, I'll have them delivered to the dock by tomorrow morning.
I need some things for the ship.
You should be more careful about the women you spend time with in port. Come back later and it will be ready.
Can you make some new poultices for my...you know?
Kynareth preserve us.
Back so soon Xander?
We've got a murderer on the loose...
What...? Whoever did this, you're dead. You hear me? Dead!
Kill one of us, and three more will take their place. The Forsworn are unstoppable.
Your body lies dead, but your soul has joined our ranks...
Freshly killed. Whoever did this is still close... and will suffer for their insolence.
Damn. We've got trouble.
Well now, this is unexpected...
I'll claw the eyes out of whoever did this!
Dead! Someone's going to pay for this.
No...
Hsss! Dead. Who did this?
Dammit. This will not stand!
What...? No... No, this can't be happening...
A...a body! How absolutely terrible.
Murdered? By Azura, who would do such a thing?
What? Someone will suffer for this injustice.
I'm going to find whoever did this, and when I do...
By Ysmir! There's a killer about.
Hmph. Looks like we got a cutthroat skulkin' around...
Man gives bards a bad name.
We Nords have a long history as warrior poets. I am proud to count myself among them.
But Mikael... He sings only for gold and the affections of wenches. He dishonors our traditions.
Shop at Belethor's for the best prices and selection. He pays me to say that.
I work for Belethor, at the general goods store.
Come by Belethor's shop, at the market. I'm sure we've got something you need.
Don't mean to be rude, but I'm too busy to talk right now.
Dovahkiin, you call yourself? Arrogant mortal.
Bahloki nahkip sillesejoor.
I will feast on your soul in Sovngarde!
You are no match for me, joor!
Paarthurnax is weak. I am strong.
My time has come again.
Maar saraan ko Sovngarde.
You are no match for me, Dovahkiin.
I have feasted on many greater than you.
You will pay for your defiance!
My teeth to your neck, Dovahkiin!
And they call me a savage. Least I don't leave my trash lying around...
Not a chance. Not for this. You're going to the Cidhna Mine for the rest of your days.
Men and women join the war, they travel far on these roads to spill each other's blood.
Falkreath's warriors always return, one way or another.
Wouldn't surprise me. They've done nothing to help in the fight for Skyrim's freedom.
Those Thalmor are elves too. I bet they're working together.
Maybe I should round up some men and take us a few prisoners to interrogate.
They're parasites. They're living in our city, under our protection, but what do they do for us? Nothing!
I know the high king invited them here, but he didn't ask me or anyone else first. Maybe he should have.
It was difficult at first. The Nords of this city are, at best, suspicious of outsiders.
But in time, I made the right friends and proved myself useful enough that they don't give me trouble anymore.
The Dark Elves are too proud and naive to understand the way things truly are, and so they continue to dwell in that slum.
I'm a Dark Elf and I live in Windhelm, so yes, I live in the Gray Quarter.
You must be new around here, or you'd know they don't let my kind live anywhere outside that slum.
Nothing new there. Most of the Nords living in Windhelm don't care much for us, but Rolff is the worst by far.
He likes to get drunk and walk around the Gray Quarter yelling insults at us in the small hours of the morning. A real charmer, that one.
Did you hear all that noise outside?
You there. If you're here to trade, step up to the counter.
It's just the market rabble. We should get back to work.
Sit back and enjoy the ride.
Don't be long. I can't wait around forever.
That's fine.
Where do you want to go?
Did you need something?
Ready to go?
Whoa!
You'd be wasted as a regular soldier. I have special plans for you. You'll be of greater use to me with greater flexibility.
Make your way to our hidden military camp in The Pale. Rikke will have important tasks for you, and will need you when we reclaim the capital.
I suspect you'll be of greater use to us with greater freedom, so you're free to engage the Imperials as you see fit.
But I also want you to find our hidden camp in Falkreath. Galmar will have special tasks for you, and will need you when we liberate the capital.
You're needed in Winterhold. Report to our hidden camp there.
You're needed in Hjaalmarch. There's much to be done.
I'm promoting you to Quaestor.
Take this blade, a symbol of your new station and responsibility. I'll keep an eye on your progress. The Empire rewards excellence, and so do I.
Battles are won by trained and disciplined men. Wars are won by talented and exceptional individuals.
I'm raising you to Praefect. Take this blade, as a gift and symbol of your new rank. You've earned it.
You've come a long way. I don't think we could do without you. I'm hereby raising you to Tribune. Congratulations.
Here, take this gift as a sign of the Empire's appreciation, and my own.
I have come to rely heavily upon you. It is only fitting that you join the upper echelon. I am hereby elevating you to the rank of Legate.
On behalf of the Empire, and myself, please accept this gift along with your new rank.
Congratulations.
However, I'm disappointed to hear that you fled from the field of battle. We do not reward cowardice in the Legion. I expect better of you.
We'll call you Ice-Veins now, for the thick blood of our land has seeped into your heart.
Here, take this. It's an Imperial officer's sword. A fitting weapon to use against our enemy.
Some brothers have taken to calling you Bone-Breaker, evidence of your ferocity and determination. A fitting name. So we shall all call you thus.
The war demands so much from us, and we give all we have to it and to the people... but I've kept a little something to offer you in appreciation.
Please take this on behalf of all the brothers and sisters.
And I need capable and loyal warriors close at hand. So I'd like to offer you a home in Windhelm. Speak with my steward who'll make the arrangements.
Multitudes of our enemies are buried in an avalanche of pain and retribution delivered by your hands. I shall call you Snow-Hammer now.
You show great passion for battle, and compassion for those we fight for. You are becoming indispensable to our cause.
We do what we do out of love for our brothers and sisters. Here, take this as a token of such love reciprocated.
You've become a true hero of Skyrim. I number you among my kin. You shall now be known as Stormblade.
The love of the land and her people flows from your heart, even as death to her enemies flows from your hands.
On behalf of the sons and daughters of Skyrim, on behalf of all that is righteous and true, take this token of our appreciation for your service.
But I'm disappointed to hear you ran away from the battle. The gods do not reward cowardice. Nor do I. I expect better from you.
Sorry, a girl can't be too careful out here.
Like I said, name's Angi and I've been living out here for a couple of years now.
I moved here from Helgen after my family was killed.
At the time I couldn't stand to be around anyone. They all felt sorry for me and I didn't want them to. It was my problem, not theirs.
Name's Angi and if you try anything stupid, I won't hesitate to put an arrow in your head.
It's not often I get visitors out here. You lost, stranger?
Two Imperial drunks who thought they were above the law.
Don't be like them. Don't feel sorry for me.
Hey! Watch what you're doing.
My money's on the big one.
I'm staying out of this.
Please, someone do something!
This should be good.
Get 'em! Kill 'em!
Ah!
Why are they hurting each other?
I could also do some good for a change. Sure, I'd go with you.
I suppose it would be better than sitting around thinking about all this.
Gray-Mane or Battle-Born?
New in town, huh? Whiterun's got two clans, both old and both respected.
Difference is, the Gray-Manes turned their backs on the Empire and we Battle-Borns stayed loyal.
So I'll ask again, Gray-Mane or Battle-Born?
Then I say well met, friend. I could tell you were a sharp one the moment I laid eyes on you.
Then you're either a Stormcloak sympathizer or a fool. Either way, you're no friend of mine.
Got stones in your ears? I asked what side you're on, Gray-Mane or Battle-Born!
Sooner or later, we all have to choose a side.
There's a whole college up in Solitude where they teach the arts. Song. Lute-playing. Poetry.
I studied there myself. Had to leave over a misunderstanding with a headmistress. Well, more a misunderstanding with her husband.
You're short on coin.
I don't think so.
I set up those targets a long time ago. I shoot at them from time to time. Don't want to get rusty with my bow.
That I am. You know how to use one?
They say that these ancient Nordic burial mounds are sometimes impenetrable.
Fortunately, these doors have a weakness if you know how to exploit it. Quite simple, really.
Quite simple really, I don't know what the fuss is about these locks. All it takes is a bit of know-how and a lot of skill.
Without the matching claw, they're normally impossible to open. And since I'm certain Karliah already did away with it, we're on our own.
That should do it. After you.
Karliah's close, I'm certain of it. Now let's get moving.
Ah, it's one of the infamous Nordic puzzle doors. How quaint.
Hmm. This one doesn't look too difficult.
Gal like you needs a warhorse, I reckon.
Hey, ugly!
I'll cut right to it. You murdered the old woman in the orphanage. You owe the Dark Brotherhood a kill. I've come to collect.
One of these poor sods has a contract out on their life. Which one is it? Any idea? Make your choice. Make your kill. And you get to walk away.
Suit yourself, but you better decide what you want to do about this arrest. No one walks away for free.
Done. Now move along, before you get me in trouble.
Well... maybe just this once. But there's still a price on your head. Better make yourself scarce, or we'll have to do this little dance again.
Only inside the gates. Much of Falkreath Hold is wilderness and there's plenty of trouble to be had.
Kust is responsible for the cemetery here in Falkreath. I take care of the shrine.
The number of graves in this cemetery never decreases.
War is not new to Falkreath. You have only to stand in our cemetery to see that.
Dengeir deserves more respect than his nephew, Jarl Siddgeir, affords him.
Without birth there is no life, without death there is only stasis. Such is the truth of Arkay.
Strangers like you bring war. I've seen enough war.
Show respect while you're here and you and I will be fine.
You have the look of a killer.
Arkay helps us find peace with death.
Indara and I like to keep to ourselves and run our farm.
My wife, Indara, has a gift with animals. It makes her a better farmer than anyone I've ever seen.
Runil has been a true friend to my wife and I in our time of need.
This war isn't needed. Imperials like my wife can be hard to handle, but we Nords could work with them. I should know.
I don't know if the war is Arkay's will or man's, but it's none of my doings.
My husband Mathies and I tend Corpselight Farm.
Falkreath is a hard place and tending a farm here is thankless work.
Mathies is a kind and wise man. The Nords could use more like him. So could the Imperials.
How could people see all the graves in Falkreath and still want to make war?
Only a hearty soul travels the road these days.
The war bleeds Skyrim. It bleeds her dry and I think none of us are the better for it.
Men and women join the war, they travel far on these roads to spill each other's blood.
Falkreath's warriors always return, one way or another.
Someone new, perhaps someone looking for me to mix something up for them?
If either these Nords or Imperials had some red in their blood this war would be over.
Falkreath's warriors always return, one way or another.
A hearty welcome to Falkreath! All of Skyrim's true sons are welcome here!
Stay out of my way, boot.
I can't believe we let provincials like you wander Skyrim.
That Valga Vinicia is a gossip and a troublemaker.
You'll find my brother Solaf and I are the only true Nords in Falkreath.
Can I get you something?
Don't mind Bolund. He's young and gets riled easily.
Looking for something?
What do you need, stranger?
Shor's bones! A handsome man in Falkreath.
You're going to have the men around here wrapped around your finger in no time.
Anything I can get you?
Can I get you a drink?
Welcome to Dead Man's Drink.
In Falkreath if I don't know it, it's not worth knowing.
I escaped fighting in Cyrodiil only to have it track me down again in Skyrim.
From maid to steward. I can hardly believe it. I hope I'm up to the task.
I'm sorry, I should really go now.
I can't really help you, I'm just Dengeir's maid.
Be mindful. My maid Tekla has been known to pass information to Siddgeir.
My nephew has always been handed things, even the title of jarl.
Stendarr protect Falkreath until Skyrim's sons rise.
Enemies and ears. Both are everywhere.
The war has changed Falkreath, even our neighbors can be our enemies.
Nenya's history of service is impeccable. She truly is the Steward of Falkreath.
Falkreath's cemetery reminds each of us that war is not new here.
Being Housecarl of Falkreath is an honor.
Dengeir was a good jarl in his day. Even if his day has passed.
I'm the steward here. I serve under Jarl Siddgeir and Jarl Dengeir before him.
Siddgeir can sometimes act too quickly, but he does listen to Helvard and I.
For Falkreath and I this is just one more war in a long line.
Stand aside. I don't need to speak to you.
Bolund speaks much about the war and the Stormcloaks, but he knows who provides.
The Stormcloaks want freedom but don't understand its price.
There are opportunities everywhere. Why don't you go find them?
No time, I have business to conduct.
Unless you intend to bury someone this isn't the place you're looking for, traveler.
Falkreath was once full of wanderers and warriors like yourself. Those were better days.
Skyrim's history of war is well documented here in Falkreath's graveyard.
My brother Dengeir can be... difficult. Take his words with a grain of salt.
While I appreciate the sentiment, I still have to decline.
Being an admirer, I'm sure you can appreciate the need to keep my research a secret.
Do you realize that at a snap of my fingers I can bring the entire Markarth city guard to my defense?
You best rethink this course of action or you may find yourself on the executioner's block.
If you need a bow, there's one on the rack to the left of the door.
Already did. Part of the reason I'm living out here now. Thanks for offering, though.
I'd give you a bow, but it looks like you've already taken it from the rack.
An outsider, eh? Never heard of the Companions?
An order of warriors. We are brothers and sisters in honor.
And we show up to solve problems if the coin is good enough.
Certainly not. But a true warrior would have relished the opportunity to take on a giant.
That's why I'm here with my shield-brothers.
Well nobody asked you. If you think you're better than we are, go talk to Kodlak Whitemane.
See what a warrior of true mettle is like.
Ha. Another milk drinker crying about their effort.
Glory only awaits those who triumph.
And as Companions, our glory must always be hard-fought.
Not for me to say. You'll have to talk to Kodlak Whitemane up in Jorrvaskr.
The old man's got a good sense for people. He can look in your eyes and tell your worth.
If you go to him, good luck.
You handle yourself well. You could make for a decent shield-brother.
You handle yourself well. You could make for a decent shield-sister.
Well, that's taken care of. No thanks to you.
I am! I am! But not just my mother. Our mother, hmm? The Night Mother! Oh yes!
But you disappeared! You were there... then you weren't! Did you mean to help poor Cicero or not? I'm left to wonder...
Ah, it doesn't matter. What's done is done, right? Cicero is here! As is the Night Mother! Joy of joys!
Here to shame an old woman? Leave me be.
But... stealing the boss' sword? Did he have a death wish?
Damn it, not now!
Ra'jirr was always dragging her into things.
But... Eisa? She's smarter than that.
Who knows? The cat was crazy. She was a fool to trust him.
We've talked about composure, speed, and precision.
Now it's time to use all three at once.
However, with such a long shot, it's more than likely that's the only shot you'll be able to take before your prey runs off.
I'll count to three and then say "go." You have ten seconds to hit each of the four targets. Draw string and get ready.
Okay, your turn. Try and hit the target way in the back, far behind the other three. Remember to take your time and make your shot count.
You did it! All four targets in ten seconds. Nice job.
Often times you won't be able to get as close to your prey as you'd like and you'll have to settle with a long shot.
When I tell you to, try and hit all four targets in ten seconds.
I'm warning you now, this challenge will be quite difficult.
Try and hit the far target.
Nice job hitting those targets.
Excellent. Follow me to the targets and we'll begin.
One, two, three...
Go!
Time's up! Better luck next time.
Here, take these. Looks like you're running low.
Listen to me and you might learn something.
We've talked about speed and composure. Now we'll go over precision.
If you find yourself in such a situation, then you need to make your shot count.
Don't rush your shot. Crouch down, get comfortable, and take aim.
Shoot the far target.
Shoot the far target.
I seem to have more chores for the Winking Skeever each day. I still have a delivery to do too.
Liar!
As long as they're playing instruments they're passable. You don't want to hear them sing.
Greta is taking it hardest. She won't go to the Temple of Divines anymore.
A little late in the day for your rounds Sorex.
Traitor!
Speaking of sounding good, I've heard a couple of your classmates on the street recently.
I'm sorry about Roggvir. It's a shame, I liked him.
Thank you, Addvar, I am picky about what I eat these days.
Shhhhhh...
Salmon sounds good today.
Sorry, I need to pay attention so I can remember this.
A war veteran should be respected.
Thanks, Aia. That means a lot.
Glad to hear it, Aia.
Stand back. Interrupting an execution is a serious crime.
Fresh fish sounds like just the thing today, Addvar.
Cut him down!
I appreciate the thought though.
Roggvir always talked about his honor. Seems to me honorable men don't get executed.
A sale's a sale but I have to know... why are you buying something for Noster?
None of what he collected tasted as good as your Spiced Wine, Evette.
I'm still finding your Spiced Wine to be the best drink I've ever tasted.
Booooo.
Excuse me, I'm trying to watch a traitor to the Emperor lose his head.
I am buying a fish for Noster. He likes fish.
Rum from Hammerfell, mulled wine from Daggerfall and the like.
Booooo.
I don't think he would have wanted your absolution, Rorlund. He worshipped Talos.
A traitor right up to the end.
